verdict.answer_first: A gets straight to the point with 'Three real problems, roughly in order of severity:', while B opens with conversational filler 'Solid as a sketch, but...'.
verdict.decisiveness: A provides a single, complete code block that integrates all its recommended fixes, whereas B provides a partial code fix and describes other necessary changes in prose.
verdict.fabrication: A invents a specific function signature `canView(req.user, req.params.id)` to illustrate its point.
verdict.actionability: A is more actionable because its single code block is a complete, drop-in fix for the most critical issues; B's code is incomplete and requires me to integrate the authorization logic myself.
